name: From Alzira to Simat de la Valldigna via the Natural Path of l'Antic Trenet
name_original: Desde Alzira hasta Simat de la Valldigna por el Camino Natural de l'Antic Trenet
region_intro: Touring cycling around Alzira offers diverse landscapes, from the fertile plains of the Xúquer River to the hills of the Sierra de Corbera. The region features expansive orange groves, lush rice fields, and natural parks like Murta i la Casella. Routes often follow riverbanks and old railway lines, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Best touring cycling routes around Alzira The most popular touring cycling route is From Alzira to Simat de la Valldigna via the Natural Path of l'Antic Trenet, a 27.0 miles (43.5 km) trail that takes 2 hours 54 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a former railway line, offering a gentle gradient through varied scenery. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Greenway of the Old Little Train – Beautiful enclosed section loop from Alzira, a difficult 29.3 miles (47.2 km) path. This route includes sections of the old railway, traversing agricultural lands and offering views of the surrounding hills. Local touring cyclists also love the Rafelguaraf-Barxeta – Beautiful enclosed section loop from Alzira, a 33.6 miles (54.1 km) trail leading through agricultural landscapes and rural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 36 minutes. Touring cycling around Alzira is defined by routes along the Xúquer River, through extensive orange groves, and past the natural features of the Murta i la Casella natural park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverbank rides to more challenging excursions into the hills. The routes in Alzira are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from 58 reviews. More than 1,100 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alzira's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Alzira?
A: Alzira offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 50 different tours available. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy rides to more challenging excursions through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Alzira?
A: Touring cycling routes in Alzira feature diverse terrain. You'll find serene rides along the &lt;strong&gt;Xúquer Riverbank&lt;/strong&gt;, passing through expansive orange groves and lush rice fields. Other routes venture into the hills of the Sierra de Corbera, offering more challenging climbs and descents. Many paths also follow old railway lines, providing gentler gradients.

Q: What natural features can I see while cycling in Alzira?
A: While cycling in Alzira, you can explore significant natural features such as the &lt;strong&gt;Xúquer River&lt;/strong&gt;, with its picturesque riverbanks. The region is also known for its vast orange groves and rice fields, which create a beautiful agricultural landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4462728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt; within the Parque Natural Municipal La Murta y la Casella offers lush forests and unique flora.

Q: Are there historical sites or landmarks along the touring cycling routes?
A: Yes, Alzira's routes often pass by historical points of interest. You can explore the ruins of the 14th-century Nuestra Señora de la Murta Fortified Monastery within the Murta Valley. The historic center of Alzira, known as La Vila, features medieval streets and remnants of the Town Wall. Slightly further afield, the magnificent &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5608443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Valldigna&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant historical attraction accessible by bike.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alzira?
A: Yes, Alzira offers several easy touring cycling routes perfect for beginners. These routes typically feature minimal elevation gain and follow gentler paths, often along riverbanks or converted railway lines. For example, the "Ribera del Xúquer Loop from Alzira" is an easier option at under 50 km.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders will find several challenging touring cycling routes in Alzira. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31461698" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rafelguaraf-Barxeta – Beautiful enclosed section loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 54.1 km route with significant elevation changes. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38225098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Walls of Alzira – Font de Tormos loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt;, which is a difficult 34.3 km path.

Q: Is it possible to reach touring cycling trailheads in Alzira using public transport?
A: Yes, Alzira is well-connected by public transport, making it convenient for cyclists. You can bring your bikes on commuter trains from Valencia directly to Alzira, providing easy access to various starting points for touring cycling routes, including those leading to areas like the Murta i la Casella natural park.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Alzira?
A: Many of Alzira's touring cycling routes are suitable for families, especially those that follow old railway lines or riverbanks, which tend to have flatter, well-maintained surfaces. These routes offer a safe and enjoyable experience for all ages, allowing families to explore the scenic landscapes together.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Alzira?
A: The touring cycling routes in Alzira are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.3 stars from 58 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from the peaceful riverbanks and orange groves to the more challenging climbs in the hills, and the opportunity to discover both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alzira?
A: The best time for touring cycling in Alzira is generally during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder and more pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the cooler, potentially wetter days of winter. This allows for comfortable exploration of the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Alzira?
A: Yes, Alzira offers several excellent circular touring cycling routes. These loops allow you to start and finish in the same location, making logistics easier. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19419132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Júcar River Levee – Cullera Lighthouse loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 69.3 km route that provides a scenic round trip.
tips: A very nice road, mostly in good condition, running along the old train route that linked Carcaixent with Gandía and Denia and which today allows reaching Tavernes beach. Some sectors of the route are shared with secondary roads, although most of it runs along gravel lanes exclusively for cycling or hiking. It is a path without technical difficulty, ideal to enjoy the landscape, which is very beautiful in itself!

Wonderful Cistercian-style monastery founded in 1298 by Jaume II el Just, one of the historical jewels that remain from the ancient Kingdom of Valencia.
Since April 2022 it has been open to the public and deserves to be visited. Inside the enclosure there are ruins of the various buildings that were part of the old monastery and that today are partly under recovery work.
It is a very quiet and beautiful place a few meters from the town of Simat that also deserves to be included in the tour and has many hiking trails.
Nice and quiet place in the shade. Some waste is needed but those who respect nature take what they bring!

The layout of the natural path takes advantage of all the rural paths between the private farms and crosses the CV-50 again directly at the old Aguas Vivas halt, built to serve an old children's hospital to cure tuberculosis. At present, the hospital attends numerous medical specialties and various differential services. In the same place is the Convent of Santa María de Aguas Vivas, from the 14th century, privately owned, included in the Route of the Monasteries (GR-236), a 90-kilometre hiking itinerary between the towns of Gandía and Alzira, visiting five monasteries of historical and artistic interest and several nature reserves.
It is part of the greenway that recovers the old layout of the "vineyard" between Carcaixent and Gandia.
